insight: In the 2026 FIFA World Cup, South Africa's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the two breaks they've had, resulting in a slight decline in performance with a mean change of -0.050 minutes per minute. When they didn't press before the break, which happened twice, they still experienced a slight decline in performance, averaging a change of -0.050 minutes per minute, indicating that the breaks didn't provide a significant boost to their gameplay. This suggests that South Africa struggles to capitalize on hydration breaks to shift the momentum in their favor, and instead, often sees a minor drop in performance. Overall, the hydration breaks have not been a significant factor in South Africa's ability to turn the tide of a match.
